title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC made their NWSL debut with a 1-0 loss to Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium.
The match saw a record-tying eight yellow cards, with Legacy's Bianca St-Georges receiving a red card.
More than 30,000 fans attended, highlighting the growing Boston vs. New York rivalry in women's soccer.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, celebrating their 30th anniversary and St. Patrick's Day.
The band criticized the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in their song 'Citizen I.C.E.' during the performance.
The concert featured opening acts from Boston-based bands The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon to support Joslin Diabetes Center.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at 11 and was inspired by Joslin's support and resources.
Running for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health, Bennett aims to raise awareness and funds for diabetes care and research.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, from Springfield, is now the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history with her fifth nod.
Carter could become the first Black person to win three Oscars if she wins the Best Costume Design for 'Sinners'.
She uses Massachusetts resources for costume design, sourcing materials from places like the Brimfield Antique Marketplace.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face potential layoffs of over 200 staff if a property-tax override is rejected by voters.
The district requires $18.5 million over three years to maintain its current staffing and programs, officials report.
A failed override could lead to larger class sizes and reduced courses, significantly impacting school operations.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act would ban sales of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops to curb reliance on puppy mills.
The legislation encourages pet stores to collaborate with shelters for adoptions and addresses insurance and housing barriers.
The Massachusetts Legislature is considering the PETS Act, aimed at enhancing pet welfare and accessibility for residents.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts officials urge water conservation as drought conditions worsen despite heavy winter snowfall.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts are now under Level 3 Critical Drought, the second-highest drought classification.
Snowmelt provides only 2.5 inches of water, insufficient to restore dry rivers, lakes, and aquifers.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph discusses women obscuring boyfriends on Instagram to challenge traditional relationship norms on the Love Letters podcast.
Joseph's viral essay questions societal expectations about centering men in women's personal identities, sparking online debates.
Chanté Joseph explores the cultural critique of heterosexuality, encouraging women to redefine their relationship priorities.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O’Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides a medically safe environment for children with serious conditions, like Reagan Carey, to have fun.
Reagan Carey, a cancer survivor, once attended the camp and now volunteers as a counselor.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles have decided not to trade A.J. Brown to the Patriots at this time, despite ongoing interest.
Trade discussions could resume around June 1, when salary cap relief for the Eagles might make a deal more feasible.
The Patriots have recently signed WR Romeo Doubs, possibly affecting their pursuit and offers for Brown.
